content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 10, 1992


Fred Huebscher
The Political Scientists
1523 No. Vista St.
Los Angeles, CA 90046

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-92-100

Dear Mr. Huebscher:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Since your questions
are hypothetical in nature, we provide the following informal
response pursuant to Regulation 18329(c)(4).2/

QUESTIONS

1. A candidate or ballot measure committee pays a slate
mailer organization to include the candidate or ballot measure on
a slate mailer delivered to one specific county. Later, for
various reasons, the slate mailer organization, without receiving
payment or authorization, includes the candidate or measure on
slate mailers delivered to other counties. Are the mailers sent
to the other counties required to include an asterisk next to the
name of the candidate or measure?

2. Under the same situation described in Question 1, would
the advice be different if the candidate or committee authorized
the slate mailer organization to include the candidate or measure
on certain slate mailers for free?3/


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

3/ In a telephone conversation on February 28, 1992, you asked
me to include this question in this advice letter.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Huebscher, Fred
April 10, 1992
Page 2




3.  If a slate mailer organization receives a payment from a
third party for the purpose of supporting or opposing a specific
candidate or measure on a slate mailer, should an asterisk be
included next to the name of the candidate or measure?

CONCLUSIONS

1.     An asterisk should not appear next to the name of a
candidate or ballot measure included in a slate mailer unless the
slate mailer organization has received a payment from the
candidate or ballot measure committee or from a third party at the
behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee.

2.     A candidate's or ballot measure committee's
authorization to place the name of the candidate or measure on a
slate mailer for free does not trigger the placement of an
asterisk, even if the candidate or committee paid to be included
on another slate mailer.

3.     Whether an asterisk should appear next to the name of
the candidate or measure under the circumstances described in
Question 3 will depend upon whether the payments to the slate
mailer organization are in-kind contributions to the candidate or
ballot measure committee or are independent expenditures. An
asterisk is required for in-kind contributions and is not required
for independent expenditures.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires slate mailer organizations to comply with
certain identification and disclaimer requirements on slate
mailings. (Section 84305.5.) Section 84305.5(a)(4) states:

     (4) Each candidate and each ballot measure which
has paid to appear in the slate mailer is
designated by an *. Any candidate or ballot
measure which has not paid to appear in the slate
mailer is not designated by an *.

(Emphasis added.)

In addition, an asterisk is required on a slate mailer when a
payment is received from another person at the behest of a
candidate or a committee supporting or opposing a ballot measure
which has been included in a slate mailer. Section 84305.5(b)

∙ Huescher, Fred
April 10, 1992
Page 3


     For purposes of the designations required by
paragraph (4) of subdivision (a), the payment of
any sum made reportable by the provisions of
subdivision (c) of Section 84219 by or at the
behest of a candidate or committee, whose name or
position appears in the mailer, to the slate mailer
organization, shall constitute a payment to appear,
requiring the * designation.  Such payment shall
also be deemed to constitute authorization to
appear in the mailer.

     An asterisk must appear next to the name of each candidate
and each ballot measure included in a slate mailer for which the
slate mailer organization has received a payment from the
candidate or ballot measure committee or at the behest of the
candidate or ballot measure committee.  When a slate mailer
organization places the name of a candidate or a ballot measure on
a slate mailer for free, the asterisk should not be included, even
if authorization has been received from the candidate or ballot
measure committee or the candidate or committee has paid to appear
on other slate mailers prepared by the slate mailer organization.

     A payment made at the behest of a candidate or committee is a
contribution.  (Section 82015.)  A payment is made at the behest
of a candidate or committee if it is made under the control of, in
the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with, or at the request or suggestion of the candidate or
committee.  (Regulation 18215(b).)  A payment for a communication
that supports or opposes a candidate or measure but which is not
made to or at the behest of the candidate or a committee
supporting or opposing the ballot measure is an independent
expenditure.  (Section 82031.)

     When a slate mailer organization receives payment from a
third party for the purpose of including a candidate or ballot
measure on a slate mailer, inclusion or the asterisk will depend
upon whether the third party's payment was made at the behest of
the candidate or ballot measure committee or was made as an
independent expenditure.  An asterisk is required when the slate
mailer organization receives a payment which was made at the
behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee.  Such
payments also must be reported as in-kind contributions by the
candidate or committee.  An asterisk is not required for payments
which are not made at the behest of the candidate or committee and
are, therefore, independent expenditures.

     If a slate mailer organization receives a third party payment
for the purpose of including a candidate or measure on a slate
mailer and does not know whether the payment was made at the
behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee, the

Huebscher, Fred
April 10, 1992
Page 4


organization should inquire so that asterisks may be properly
included on the slate mailer.

     Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        [illegible]
                                        By: Lynda Cassady
                                            Political Reform Consultant

sections.question:
1. A candidate or ballot measure committee pays a slate mailer organization to include the candidate or ballot measure on a slate mailer delivered to one specific county. Later, for various reasons, the slate mailer organization, without receiving payment or authorization, includes the candidate or measure on slate mailers delivered to other counties. Are the mailers sent to the other counties required to include an asterisk next to the name of the candidate or measure?

2. Under the same situation described in Question 1, would the advice be different if the candidate or committee authorized the slate mailer organization to include the candidate or measure on certain slate mailers for free?

1, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)

3/ In a telephone conversation on February 28, 1992, you asked me to include this question in this advice letter.

3. If a slate mailer organization receives a payment from a third party for the purpose of supporting or opposing a specific candidate or measure on a slate mailer, should an asterisk be included next to the name of the candidate or measure?
sections.conclusion:
1. An asterisk should not appear next to the name of a candidate or ballot measure included in a slate mailer unless the slate mailer organization has received a payment from the candidate or ballot measure committee or from a third party at the behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee.

2. A candidate's or ballot measure committee's authorization to place the name of the candidate or measure on a slate mailer for free does not trigger the placement of an asterisk, even if the candidate or committee paid to be included on another slate mailer.

3. Whether an asterisk should appear next to the name of the candidate or measure under the circumstances described in Question 3 will depend upon whether the payments to the slate mailer organization are in-kind contributions to the candidate or ballot measure committee or are independent expenditures. An asterisk is required for in-kind contributions and is not required for independent expenditures.
sections.analysis:
The Act requires slate mailer organizations to comply with certain identification and disclaimer requirements on slate mailings. (Section 84305.5.) Section 84305.5(a)(4) states:

(4) Each candidate and each ballot measure which has paid to appear in the slate mailer is designated by an *. Any candidate or ballot measure which has not paid to appear in the slate mailer is not designated by an *.

(Emphasis added.)

In addition, an asterisk is required on a slate mailer when a payment is received from another person at the behest of a candidate or a committee supporting or opposing a ballot measure which has been included in a slate mailer. Section 84305.5(b) states:

For purposes of the designations required by paragraph (4) of subdivision (a), the payment of any sum made reportable by the provisions of subdivision (c) of Section 84219 by or at the behest of a candidate or committee, whose name or position appears in the mailer, to the slate mailer organization, shall constitute a payment to appear, requiring the * designation. Such payment shall also be deemed to constitute authorization to appear in the mailer.

An asterisk must appear next to the name of each candidate and each ballot measure included in a slate mailer for which the slate mailer organization has received a payment from the candidate or ballot measure committee or at the behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee. When a slate mailer organization places the name of a candidate or a ballot measure on a slate mailer for free, the asterisk should not be included, even if authorization has been received from the candidate or ballot measure committee or the candidate or committee has paid to appear on other slate mailers prepared by the slate mailer organization.

A payment made at the behest of a candidate or committee is a contribution. (Section 82015.) A payment is made at the behest of a candidate or committee if it is made under the control or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with, or at the request or suggestion of the candidate or committee. (Regulation 18215(b).) A payment for a communication that supports or opposes a candidate or measure but which is not made to or at the behest of the candidate or a committee supporting or opposing the ballot measure is an independent expenditure. (Section 82031.)

When a slate mailer organization receives payment from a third party for the purpose of including a candidate or ballot measure in a slate mailer, inclusion of the asterisk will depend upon whether the third party's payment was made at the behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee or was made as an independent expenditure. An asterisk is required when the slate mailer organization receives a payment which was made at the behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee. Such payments also must be reported as in-kind contributions by the candidate or committee. An asterisk is not required for payments which are not made at the behest of the candidate or committee and are, therefore, independent expenditures.

If a slate mailer organization receives a third party payment for the purpose of including a candidate or measure on a slate mailer and does not know whether the payment was made at the behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee, the

organization should inquire so that asterisks may be properly included on the slate mailer.

Later, for various reasons, the slate mailer organization, without receiving payment or authorization, includes the candidate or measure on slate mailers delivered to other counties. Are the mailers sent to the other counties required to include an asterisk next to the name of the candidate or measure?

2. Under the same situation described in Question 1, would the advice be different if the candidate or committee authorized the slate mailer organization to include the candidate or measure on certain slate mailers for free?

1, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)

3/ In a telephone conversation on February 28, 1992, you asked me to include this question in this advice letter.

3. If a slate mailer organization receives a payment from a third party for the purpose of supporting or opposing a specific candidate or measure on a slate mailer, should an asterisk be included next to the name of the candidate or measure?

CONCLUSION: 1. An asterisk should not appear next to the name of a candidate or ballot measure included in a slate mailer unless the slate mailer organization has received a payment from the candidate or ballot measure committee or from a third party at the behest of the candidate or ballot measure committee.

2. A candidate's or ballot measure committee's authorization to place the name of the candidate or measure on a slate mailer for free does not trigger the placement of an asterisk, even if the candidate or committee paid to be included on another slate mailer.

3. Whether an asterisk should appear next to the name of the candidate or measure under the circumstances described in Question 3 will depend upon whether the payments to the slate mailer organization are in-kind contributions to the candidate or ballot measure committee or are independent expenditures. An asterisk is required for in-kind contributions and is not required for independent expenditures.
